ports.name: Harvest Caye, Belize
ports.overview: NCL's private island destination in southern Belize. A purpose-built resort island with a beautiful beach, large pool complex, water sports, and a zip line over the water. Much calmer and more controlled than a typical port stop — perfect for a relaxed beach day.

top_activities.description: White sand beach and calm lagoon water — excellent for swimming and snorkeling. Snorkel gear rentable on the island. Reef close to shore.
Large multi-level pool with swim-up bar. Usually less crowded than the main ship pool on port days since many guests stay on island.
Zip line over the water from the island — one of Harvest Caye's signature attractions. Book early in the day as slots fill up.
top_activities.cost: ~$35-50 per person (book onboard or at the island activity desk)

top_activities.description: Kayaks, paddleboards, wave runners available for rent. Calm lagoon water ideal for paddleboarding.
Small Belize Wildlife Sanctuary on the island with local animals — good preview of Belize wildlife if not doing excursions.
top_activities.cost: Small fee or included depending on NCL package

top_activities.description: Overwater hammocks and beach cabana rentals available. Book a cabana early — they sell out.

dining.overview: Multiple dining options on the island — a restaurant/bar, beach bar, and food stands.
dining.gf_notes: The main island restaurant (Toucan's Bar & Grill) can accommodate GF requests — alert staff to gluten sensitivity. Grilled proteins, rice, and salads are generally safe. Avoid fried items. The dedicated GF phrase in Spanish is useful here even though Belize is English-speaking — many kitchen staff are bilingual.
safe_choices: rice and beans (confirm no wheat additive)
dining.tip: Haven Restaurant on the ship remains open during port days — safest fallback is returning to the ship for lunch.

practical_tips: Bring reef-safe sunscreen — required on the island and better for snorkeling
Water shoes recommended — some areas have rocky entry points
The ship tenders or docks directly at Harvest Caye — no tender anxiety
All-inclusive drink package works on the island
Get off the ship early for the best beach chairs and hammock spots
The island is small enough to walk everywhere — no need for the tram
Cell service is spotty — enjoy the disconnect

weather.expected: Hot and humid, 82-88°F (28-31°C). Mostly sunny with possible afternoon showers — typical for Belize in April.

ports.currency: USD widely accepted on the private island. No need for Belize dollars (BZD) on Harvest Caye specifically.
ports.return_to_ship: All aboard: 5:00pm. Be back by 4:30pm to be safe.

ports.name: Roatán, Honduras

booked_excursions.name: Monkeys, Sloths & Macaws
booked_excursions.description: Wildlife sanctuary tour featuring interactive experiences with spider monkeys, two-toed sloths, and scarlet macaws. Typically includes a guide-led walk through the sanctuary and photo opportunities with the animals. One of Roatán's most popular shore excursions.
booked_excursions.meeting_point: Check Freestyle Daily or excursion ticket for exact meeting point — usually at the pier or ship's gangway
tips: Wear closed-toe shoes or sturdy sandals — uneven paths
Don't wear loose dangling jewelry — monkeys are curious
Bring small bills for optional tips to guides (~$5-10 USD per person)
Apply bug spray before departing — jungle setting
Monkeys may climb on you — don't resist, it's part of the experience
Sloths are slow movers — morning sessions sometimes more active
Leave time to return to ship — noon departure means back by ~3-3:30pm

suggestions: Explore the port shopping area near the pier — jewelry, crafts, souvenirs
West Bay Beach is Roatán's famous beach (~20 min taxi from port) — stunning turquoise water
West End Village has local restaurants and shops if you want a quick explore
The pier at Mahogany Bay (NCL's port area) has its own beach and chair lift
Return to ship and decompress at the Haven pool
Quick shower and order Haven room service
Haven bar for a late afternoon cocktail

top_activities.description: Consistently ranked one of the best beaches in the Caribbean. Calm, clear water with excellent snorkeling off the beach. 15-20 min taxi ride from Mahogany Bay pier.
NCL's beach at the port — accessible via a short chair lift. Calmer option if not venturing to West Bay.
top_activities.cost: Chair lift: small fee or included with NCL package

top_activities.description: Roatán is part of the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef — world-class snorkeling. Multiple spots accessible from West Bay Beach.

dining.overview: Port area has restaurants and food stalls. Ship is the safest GF option.
dining.gf_notes: Holly should strongly prefer ship food on this port day. Street food and local restaurants have unpredictable cross-contamination. If dining ashore, choose a sit-down restaurant, not food stalls. Use the Spanish GF phrase even though English is widely spoken — kitchen staff may speak more Spanish.
safe_port_choices: grilled fish at a sit-down restaurant (confirm GF prep)
dining.tip: NCL's Mahogany Bay port area has a restaurant with NCL oversight — safer than independent port restaurants for gluten-free dietary restriction concerns.

practical_tips: USD is accepted everywhere in Roatán — no need for Honduran Lempira
Taxis are shared (collectivos) — negotiate price before getting in
Mahogany Bay is NCL's dedicated port — security is good, very tourist-friendly
Don't display expensive jewelry or electronics in the port area
Sunscreen and bug spray essential
The excursion meets at noon — don't stray too far from port in the morning

weather.expected: Hot and sunny, 84-90°F (29-32°C). April is dry season in Roatán — low rain probability.
what_to_bring: camera or phone in waterproof case

ports.currency: USD universally accepted. Honduran Lempira not needed.
ports.return_to_ship: All aboard: 5:00pm. Excursion should return by ~3:30pm.

ports.name: Costa Maya, Mexico
ports.overview: A Mexican Caribbean port on the southern Yucatán coast. The port itself is a purpose-built tourist complex with shops and restaurants. The main draw is proximity to the Chacchoben Mayan ruins (~40 min away) and the laid-back beach town of Mahahual (~5 min from port). A good day to decide based on energy levels — ruins for history, beach for relaxation.

top_activities.description: Well-preserved Mayan ceremonial site in the jungle, dating to around 200 AD. Smaller and less touristy than Chichen Itza. Taxis run directly from the port — negotiate a round-trip fare including wait time. A licensed guide at the ruins enhances the experience significantly.
top_activities.cost: Taxi round-trip ~$40-60 for the car (split between 2 = $20-30 each). Site admission ~$5 USD. Optional guide ~$10-15 per person.
tips: Go early — gets very hot and crowded by midday
Wear comfortable walking shoes — uneven jungle terrain
Bring bug spray — jungle setting
Negotiate taxi price firmly before departing (round-trip with wait time)
The ruins are climbable — great photo ops
Cash only at the site — bring small USD bills

top_activities.description: Charming laid-back beach village just 5 minutes from the port. Pedestrian-friendly malecón (boardwalk) lined with restaurants and shops. Great for a casual morning stroll, fresh ceviche (check GF), or renting a beach chair.
top_activities.cost: Free to explore. Beach chair rentals ~$5-10. Food varies.
tips: Very walkable — taxi or walk from port
Less commercial than the port complex
Good spot for souvenir shopping — local crafts, hammocks, textiles

top_activities.description: The Costa Maya port has a large open-air shopping area with jewelry, tequila, souvenirs, and a pool. If energy is low, easy to spend the morning here without leaving the port.
top_activities.cost: Free entry. Shopping at your budget.

top_activities.description: Costa Maya port complex has a large infinity pool available to cruise passengers. Good option for a relaxed beach-free swim.

dining.overview: Port complex has restaurants. Mahahual has local seafood spots.
dining.gf_notes: Mexico is generally manageable for GF — corn is the staple starch, not wheat. However, cross-contamination is still a concern in smaller kitchens. Use the Spanish GF phrase. Corn tortillas are naturally GF but may share fryers with flour tortillas.
safe_port_choices: grilled fish tacos on corn tortillas (confirm no flour)
ceviche (naturally GF — confirm no soy sauce)
guacamole with corn chips (confirm no wheat chips mixed in)
avoid: anything with mole sauce (often contains wheat)
dining.tip: The port complex restaurants cater heavily to cruise passengers and are more likely to understand GF requests than small local spots.

decision_guide.choose_ruins_if: You have energy and interest in Mayan history. Go early — depart by 8:30am for best experience.
decision_guide.choose_beach_if: You want a low-key day. Mahahual beach is beautiful and relaxing.
decision_guide.choose_stay_onboard_if: Energy is low after two consecutive port days. Haven pool with few people on port days is a genuine luxury.

practical_tips: Confirm port arrival time the night before via Freestyle Daily
Negotiate all taxi prices upfront — agree on round-trip with wait time for ruins
Port is walking distance from Mahahual — about 15-20 min walk or quick taxi
USD widely accepted; pesos fine too but not required
The port complex is modern and safe
Bring cash for ruins admission, taxi, and tips

weather.expected: Hot and sunny, 82-88°F (28-31°C). Low humidity compared to Belize. April is dry season.

ports.currency: USD accepted everywhere. Mexican Pesos (MXN) also accepted but not required.
ports.return_to_ship: All aboard: check Freestyle Daily. Typically 5:00pm. Ruins trip: depart by 8:30am, back by noon easily.

ports.name: Cozumel, Mexico
ports.overview: Mexico's largest Caribbean island and one of the world's top diving and snorkeling destinations. The port is in San Miguel de Cozumel, a lively town with excellent shopping and restaurants. For this trip, the plan leans toward staying onboard or a short port walk — after three consecutive port days, a lighter day at sea or the Haven deck is a reasonable and luxurious choice.
ports.plan_recommendation: Consider staying onboard. The Haven pool is virtually empty while the ship is docked. This is a rare opportunity for a quiet, private Haven pool day. If going ashore, keep it to a 2-3 hour shopping/lunch trip.

top_activities.description: With most passengers ashore, the Haven deck becomes exceptionally quiet. Take over the pool and deck chairs. Butler service at full attention. Casino may have good action with fewer people.

The International Pier and San Miguel malecón are walkable from the dock. Duty-free jewelry shops (especially silver and gemstones), local artisan markets, tequila tastings, and souvenir shops line the waterfront. Very walkable and safe.
top_activities.cost: Free to browse. Shopping at your budget.
tips: Silver jewelry is a Cozumel specialty — good quality at fair prices
Duty-free shops are competitive — compare before buying
Los Cinco Soles is a well-known crafts market with quality items
Tequila and vanilla are popular purchases

top_activities.description: Popular beach club about 10 minutes from port by taxi. All-inclusive day pass available. Pool, beach, water sports, open bar.
tips: Reserve in advance if considering this option
NCL drink packages generally don't extend to off-ship venues

top_activities.description: Cozumel's reefs (Palancar, Columbia, Santa Rosa) are world-famous. If motivated, a 2-hour snorkel boat tour is easy to book independently at the pier.
top_activities.cost: ~$25-40 per person for basic snorkel tour

dining.overview: Cozumel has excellent restaurants. For a quick port trip, focus on the waterfront.
dining.gf_notes: Same Mexico GF guidelines apply as Costa Maya — corn-based cuisine is your friend, confirm preparation. If staying onboard, Haven Restaurant and Haven pool food service are safest.
safe_port_choices: fish tacos on corn tortillas (confirm at restaurant)
grilled lobster or shrimp (popular in Cozumel)
dining.recommended_port_restaurant: La Cocay (upscale, GF-friendly, walk from pier) or Kinta (Mexican cuisine, known for accommodating dietary restrictions) — ask about gluten sensitivity when seated.
dining.tip: If dining onboard, today is a great day to try a specialty restaurant at lunch (check if open during port days) or order a Haven balcony lunch.

practical_tips: The pier area in Cozumel is very tourist-friendly and walkable
Taxis are readily available and set-rate — no negotiation needed (rates posted)
The island is very safe for tourists in the shopping/port district
Many shops are duty-free — good for jewelry, vanilla, and spirits
Sunrise direction from the ship is typically east-facing in Cozumel — great balcony morning
NCL drink packages work on the ship all day even while docked

weather.expected: Warm and sunny, 82-87°F (28-30°C). April is peak dry season — excellent conditions.

ports.currency: USD universally accepted in tourist areas. Pesos fine but not necessary.
ports.return_to_ship: All aboard: check Freestyle Daily. Typically 5:00pm. Port is very close to the pier — easy to return quickly.
